title: 2023 Ford F-150 — Refinish Operations: Front Door
issueSummary: When refinishing a panel two or three stage and adding two tone to the panel, the "blend" panel time does not change to account for the two tone operation. Blending is only calculating on the base refinish time. How can we get CCC to add time when blending a panel with two tone in conjunction with the main panel?
suggestedAction: Advise how to handle blend panel two tone time adjustment when a set percentage has been assigned to the blend operation?
resolution: Estimated Release Date: Closed Proposed Resolution: The Blend calculation is determined from the full refinish time of the component based on the MOTOR GTE Pages. Additional refinish stages are not considered. If additional refinish time is needed to blend a two tone panel, then a manual estimate change would be required.

title: 2015 Chrysler Town & Country — Procedure Page Issue: Front
issueSummary: R&I and O/H procedure in CEG does not address hoisting and supporting the vehicle as well as the additional operations called out in the OEM service procedures.As a form of reference I looked and the R&I time for the wheel does mention jack and support vehicle in the included operations on the same vehicle.In order to remove the front bumper, the vehicle must be raised and supported to R/I the fender liners before removing the bumper cover, to remove the liners the front wheels should be r/i. Is any steps related to raising/ suppoting the vehicle, fender liner r/i/ wheel r/i factored into the bumper labor or would all those operations be separate operations?
suggestedAction: Advise. Was hoist and support vehicle factored into the the R&I time for the front bumper cover.
resolution: Proposed Response Text: Thank you for your inquiry, Please reference previous DEG inquiry 17360 & 18609. Per p-page for front and rear bumpers, removing and installing the wheel has not been factored into the R&I Bumper labor allowance. After review, we have determined that the published labor allowance is sufficient to perform the requirements of the operation as outlined in the most current information from the vehicle manufacturer.Raise and Supporting the vehicle is NOT factored into the bumper labor.

title: 2019 Chevrolet Trax — Non-Welded Panel Operations: Rear Bumper
issueSummary: Is the time to remove the part of the rivets that stay in the panel and after those are removed the pieces that fall into the rear body/quarter panel included with the .3 to R&R guide bracket for the rear bumper?Missing Rivets from CCC database in addition holding the bumper guide. Please add PN OEM 9132667
suggestedAction: Please clarify if this is included (this applies to many vehicles)Please add missing rivets to database OEM 9132667
resolution: Estimated Release Date: Closed Proposed Resolution: MOTOR stated: After review of your concern, we have determined the following:• Per latest OEM information and VIN provided, in the Rear Bumper 2017-2022 group, Bumper & Components subgroup, the Guide Bracket Rivet (9132667) is correct as listed.• “Removal/Clean-Up” of material debris from “Drilling/Grinding” is considered a procedural step when preforming either “Drilling or Grinding.”

title: 2019 Chevrolet Trax — Non-Welded Panel Operations: Rear Bumper
issueSummary: Is the time to remove the part of the rivets that stay in the panel and after those are removed the pieces that fall into the rear body/quarter panel included with the .2 to R&R guide bracket for the rear bumper?In addition, Rivets holding the bumper guide to the body is Missing from mitchell database.
suggestedAction: Clarify if removal of the rivets that fall into the backside/ recessed area is included or not?Add rivets to database.
resolution: Proposed Response Text: Thank you for your inquiry. we have added rivets for the Bumper cover Guide in the Rear Bumper/2017-21 section PN 9132667 After review we have determined the labor allowance is sufficient to perform the requirements of the operation including the attaching hardware.Additional DEG Note: Confirming with Mitchell, Labor to replace the bumper guide bracket attached to the quarter panel does NOT INCLUDE any additional labor to remove interior trims/ components to remove the remnants left behind from the attachment fastener.

title: 2019 Chevrolet Trax — Non-Welded Panel Operations
issueSummary: Is the time to remove the part of the rivets that stay in the panel and after those are removed the pieces that fall into the rear body/quarter panel included with the .2 (1.3) to R&R guide bracket for the rear bumper?
suggestedAction: Clarify if removal of the rivets that fall into the backside/ recessed area is included or not?
resolution: Response from Audatex RFR “Retrieving the back side of the rivet is not included in our labor times. No rfr warranted at this time.”

title: 2014 Audi q8 — All Other: Front
issueSummary: Rear door concern on this car since it is now frameless door window design. Comparing to 2019 Q7 with framed doors, both vehicles appear to have same .5 labor on the rear door glass after considering included operations. Looking at procedures and updates from Audi, Q8 has additional horizontal/ lateral adjustments which is more complex than the Q7 which has a guided frame.
suggestedAction: Correct Q8 Rear door glass window labor to reflect the additional labor needed for adjustment required in the instruction erwin.
resolution: We have reviewed the current OEM service information for the GLASS,REAR DOOR P (GN 0295/0296). The labor allowance for the GLASS,REAR DOOR P (GN 0295/0296) remains unchanged as all required operations have been considered according to the OEM service information and Audatex internal labor guidelines. Per the DBRM Audatex labor includes normal adjustment and alignment for correct fit, but does not include any duplicated effort. No change warranted at this time.“Any additional adjustments needed would have to be a manual entry as we have already accounted for a normal adjustment.”

title: 2020 Cadillac ct6 — Parts: Other
issueSummary: Issues defining what a " Break Point" is. In the P pages it states that the clear coat must carried to the nearest Break Point. On this particular case the damage is to one quarter panel and the clear coat needs to be taken UP on the roof and OVER to the adjacent Pillar/Quarter panel. According to the insurance adjuster's interpretation, a gap between two panels is not necessary and the valley delineating the pillar from the roof is a proper location to break the clear coat. Clear Coat Undamaged Panel SPECIAL NOTATION: Calculations for clear coating an undamaged panel are based upon the outer surface only and should not include additions for underside, inside, or edges of the clear-coated panel. There should be no overlap deduction between refinished or clear-coated panel(s), nor should this procedure be applied towards the maximum clear coat allocation. Clear coating may be necessary for adjacent body panel(s) to nearest break point (see Refinish Clear Coat Application). The following formula may be considered in the event this type of procedure is required on an undamaged panel.
suggestedAction: We would like to have a clear definition on what a Break Point is. so that it is not left for interpretation. Thank you.
resolution: Update response: MOTOR feedback: A breakpoint would be a hard stopping point in a undetectable area that may be covered by a molding or the panel edge. The image sent would not be considered a breakpoint or panel edge. Estimated Release Date: Closed Proposed Resolution: MOTOR stated: After review, when refinishing a “Welded-On Panel” the requirement is to refinish to the nearest “Break-Point”, because creating a Blend-Line at a “Non-Break Point” may result in “Refinish Deterioration”.Your example; “Repairing the Quarter Panel and the Outer Surface of those parts will require some type of refinishing. The time necessary to perform this type of operation should be estimated after an on-the-spot evaluation of required procedure. MOTOR recommends these factors be considered before finalizing any repair cost estimate.Please note: According to the “Guide To Estimating: Refinish Clear Coat Application,” “Most major paint manufacturers recommend that when performing refinish repairs on an OEM base coat/clear coat or multistage finishes, the application of clear coat must be extended to the nearest panel edge or breakpoint to qualify for their lifetime refinish warranties.”According to the “Guide To Estimating: CLEAR COAT UNDAMAGED PANEL”, the labor allowance is established by calculating 40% of the non-overlapped “BASIC COLOR COAT APPLICATION” allowance for the same component published in the MOTOR database. It is important to emphasize this 40% formula is intended for use in only those instances when applying clear coat to a panel that is connected seamlessly to an adjacent panel that’s being refinished. Calculations for “CLEAR COAT UNDAMAGED PANEL” are based upon the outer surface only and should not include additions for underside, inside, or edges of the clear-coated panel. There should be no overlap deduction between refinished or clear-coated panel(s), nor should this procedure be applied towards the maximum clear coat allocation. The allowance for “CLEAR COAT UNDAMAGED PANEL” is based on the clear already mixed for the balance of the vehicle.According to the “Guide To Estimating: Blend allowance is calculated using 50% of the non-overlapped, base panel refinish time for the same component published in the MOTOR database. It is important to emphasize this 50% formula is intended for use in only those instances when applying color onto the initial panel and continuing onto an undamaged adjacent panel, in order to achieve proper color transition or match between the new paint and the existing paint. The blend formula is not intended for estimating time that may be required to refinish any panel with damage. It is MOTOR’s position that damaged panel estimating is a process best reserved for the judgment of an estimator / appraiser following a thorough on the spot evaluation of the specific vehicle and damage in question.MOTOR’s blend allowance includes clear coat, and our formula assumes that the color and clear have already been mixed for the balance of the vehicle. Tinting of a color would be performed during the initial preparation steps, not during the blend process and would not be included.

title: 2014 Honda CIVIC — Non-Welded Panel Operations: Quarter Panel
issueSummary: WHEN REFINISH IS ADDED ON LEFT QUARTER AND REFINISH IS ALSO ADDED TO FUEL DOOR , FUEL DOOR REFINISH SHOWS INCLUDED IN QUARTER PANEL . REGARDLESS IF THE FUEL DOOR IS REFINISHED ON OR OFF THE CAR, THE ADDITIONAL LABOR IT REQUIRES TO MASK THE POCKET PROPERLY TAKES MORE TIME. IS THIS CONSIDERED INCLUDED ?
suggestedAction: ALLOW FOR ADDITIONAL TIME TO REFINISH 1/4 PANEL WITH FUEL DOOR DUE TO MASKING OF THE POCKET AREA IF THE SURFACE MEASUREMENTS IS THE SAME FOR BOTH R AND L 1/4 PANELS . ALSO CLARIFY IF THE MASKING OF THE RECESSED AREA OF THE POCKET IS NOT INCLUDED.
resolution: Proposed Response Text: Thank you for your inquiry, Perimeter masking is included up to 36 inches, areas requiring masking within the perimeter have not been factored into the refinish allowance.

title: 2007 mazda 3 — All Other: Front bumper impact bar
issueSummary: Part needs to be refinished and database does not show any refinish time
suggestedAction: Add 1.0 refinish time to data base
resolution: DEG ResponsePlease review DEG Inquiry Number 4283 After review, MOTOR has determined that paint found on factory installed impact bars is an artifact of the factory refinish process (over-spray). Impact bars may come from the factory refinished in various stages of completeness and quality.MOTOR does not take the position that the impact bar should not be returned to pre-accident condition. However, determining the estimated work time to refinish impact bars in various stages of completeness and quality, if refinished at all, is best left up to the judgment of estimators using an on-the-spot evaluation.No changes required.

title: 2013 TOYOTA SIENNA — All Other: REAR BODY
issueSummary: PART COMES AS AN ASSEMBLY, INNER OUTER, LT AND RT SIDE, LABOR TIME DISCREPENCY, FOR PARTIAL INSTALLATION AND FULL INSTALLATION. FULL INSTALL APPEARS TO HAVE WHOLE BACK END REPLACED! WE SHOW VARIED LABOR TIMES FROM UNDER 10 HRS TO 59 HRS
resolution: IP ExplanationThe labor allowances for the full and partial replacement operations reflect the OEM service procedures for each operation. These operations reflect the OEM service procedures and these procedures should be consulted if there are questions regarding the replacement of this part. The full replacement reflects removal of both quarter panels. The left/right partial replacement reflect sectioning with the appropriate quarter panel removed. The panel rear body outer reflects just sectioning the center portion of the outer portion of the panel per the OEM service procedures. The panel, rear body inner reflects just sectioning the center portion of the inner portion of the panel per the OEM service procedures.

title: 2009 GMC SIERRA K2500 — Refinish Operations: BEDSIDE
issueSummary: PLEASE CK REFINISH LABOR TIME. TO REFINISH A BEDSIDE AND TAILGATE ON THIS FULL SIZE TRUCK VERSUS, A FORD RANGER 7FT BEDSIDE THERE IS ONLY .3 TENTHS DIFF. BETWEEN LABORS. CLEARLY THERE IS MUCH MORE SURFACE AREA ON A FULLSIZE TRUCK TO JUSTIFY MORE REFINISH ON FULL SIZE TRUCK BEDS WE SEE THIS ISSUE TIME AND TIME AGAIN.
suggestedAction: ADD MORE REFINISH TIME TO FULL SIZE BED REFINISH
resolution: Estimated Fix December 2013Estimated UM Release Date: 12/09/2013 Estimated DVD Release Date: 01-2014 MOTOR Publication Fix Date: 01-2014Proposed Resolution: MOTOR stated: After a physical review of the surface area in question, we have determined that the refinish time for the GMC Sierra Side Panel is correct. We also reviewed the refinish time for the Ranger and determined it was incorrect. An adjustment to the refinish time on the Ranger has been implemented. For the 6 foot Side Panel the refinish time has been adjusted to 2.7 hours from 3.2 hours and for the 7 foot Side Panel, the estimated refinish time has been adjusted to 2.9 hours from 3.3 hours.No changes are required for the Sierra.

issueSummary: REMOVE AND INSTALL ENGINE, CCC GIVES ONLY 6.8, ALL DATA STATES 10.0 HOURS WHIS IS MORE ACCURATE,

resolution: Estimated Fix August 2014Estimated UM Release Date: 08/15/2014 Estimated DVD Release Date: 09-2014 MOTOR Publication Fix Date: 10-2014Proposed Resolution: MOTOR stated: In the “Engine/Transaxle” groups, the estimated work time applied to the “R&I engine/trans assembly” operation has been adjusted to 9.8 hours from 6.8 hours.

issueSummary: PART NOT IN DATA BASE

title: 2012 DODGE R1500 — Refinish Operations
issueSummary: GRILLE DOESNT NOT COME SEALED OR PRIMED FROM FACTORY AND REQUIRES PAW PLASTIC PREP.
suggestedAction: REVISE CCC ONE DATA BASE FOR ALLOWED LABOR FOR UNPRIMED PART.
resolution: No ChangeEstimated Release Date: Closed Proposed Resolution: MOTOR stated: After review of the Grill group, Grille & Components subgroup, the Grille is not a flexible component like a Bumper Cover, therefor an operation to “Prep for raw plastics” would not be applicable in this situation. According to the “Guide To Estimating,” “Refinishing Procedures, Basic color coat Application,” “Included” items, “application of sealer primer” is an included operation.

title: 2014 NISSAN VERSA NOTE SV — All Other: GRILLE
issueSummary: Grills do not come in color, need to be painted per application.
suggestedAction: Change CCC to add refinish time of 1.5 hours plus clear coat time to estimating systems
resolution: IP ExplanationEstimated Release Date: Closed Proposed Resolution: MOTOR stated: After review of the Front Bumper & Grill, Hatchback group, Grill & Components subgroup the following has been determined:• The OEM “Grill assembly” OEM Part Number 623103VY0A does not require refinishing. The Grill assembly is serviced “black.”• In the Grill & Components subgroup there is a component, Grill Cover, 2014, part number 623203VY0H with an estimated refinish time of 1.3 hours.• If refinishing is required, an “on the spot evaluation” to determine estimated refinish time will be required when the estimate is being written.No change required.

The most current version of the database has a 02/08/2017 date. Select Estimate Properties on the Estimate tab to confirm your database version.

LABOR: Time is after bumper assembly is removed. Time includes Holder.”

title: 2017 Audi A4 — Non-Welded Panel Operations
issueSummary: MISSING ADD FOR PARK SENSOR OPTION
suggestedAction: PLEASE ADD "ADD FOR PARK SENSORS" UNDER ADDITIONAL OPERATIONS.
resolution: Estimated Release Date: Closed Proposed Resolution: MOTOR stated: After review of the Front Bumper groups, W/O S-LINE and W/S-LINE, Electrical Components subgroups. the following has been determined:• The Front Bumper group W/O S-LINE does not have Park Sensors in the Electrical Components subgroup and the “R&R bumper cover, add for park sensors ” option is not required.• The Front Bumper group W/S-LINE has Park Sensors in the Electrical Components subgroup and the “R&R bumper cover, add for park sensors ” is present in the Front bumper group W/S-LINE data.

title: 2012 Ford f150 — Non-Welded Panel Operations
issueSummary: the grille comes in four major parts and all the hardware is bagged in several individual bags. the time to refinish is not enough time based on the angles and area to be painted. Extensive prep labor required to get grille to paintable stage to apply, sealer, basecoat, clear coat. If proper prep is not done, then the paint will not adhere correctly.
suggestedAction: Adjust refinish labor to 2.0 hours to account for proper prep labor. Adjust the R/R labor to 2.0 hours to account for the fact the grille needs to be built and add individual labor to each component just a single part needs to be replaced.
resolution: Estimated Release Date: Closed Proposed Resolution: MOTOR stated: After review of Hood & Grille group, the following has been determined:• After review of the OEM parts information it has been determined that the inquirers supplied parts information could not be verified. The OEM parts information list the Grille as a complete assembly.• After review, it has been determined the at the estimated work time of 0.3 hours for R&I/R&R of the OEM Grille as a single component assembly is appropriate, and the estimated refinish time of 1.0 hour for any of the MOTOR listed Ford Grilles painted surrounds or paint to match surrounds is appropriate.• For further resolution of this issue, further information is required, OEM part number for the Grille and separate components, packaging with OEM part numbers, an estimate copy, and/or photographs of damaged grille.

title: 2014 Honda Accord — Procedure Page Issue: Cooling
issueSummary: Is vacuum filling radiator with special piece of equipment and extra steps to fill are required.
suggestedAction: Note that vacuum fill is NOT included with a radiator r/r or r&i
resolution: Estimated Release Date: Closed Proposed Resolution: MOTOR stated: After review of the Cooling group we’ve determined the following: 1. According to the “Guide To Estimating: RADIATOR”, “Drain, Check and Refill Fluids” and “Purge Air From System” is listed as “Included”.Please note: a. During the process of “Refill Fluids” and “Purge Air from System” the technician should “Visually Check” for leaks and “Cooling System Concerns”.b. Pressure Testing for Diagnosis of Cooling system is Not Included.c. Cooling System leak repair is Not Included.MOTOR recommends following OEM service repair information for Cooling System Leak Repair and mutually agreed upon before finalizing any repair cost estimate.

title: 2017 Audi Q7 — Missing Information: Front
issueSummary: There is a cooling fan that is located inside the headlamp housing that does not come with a new headlamp. There is no way to know that it needs to be removed from the old headlamp and installed in the new one.
suggestedAction: Make a footnote that time does or not include removing fan from inside of housing and installing in new headlamp.
resolution: Estimated UM Release Date: 06/08/2018 Estimated DVD Release Date: 07-2018Proposed Resolution: MOTOR stated: After review of the OEM service information and MOTOR Data, in the Headlamp group the following updates were made:In the Headlamp Components Xenon subgroup• The estimated work time of 0.7 hours applied to Headlamp Assembly is appropriate, however the footnote has been updated to state, “LABOR: Time includes R&I/R&R xenon bulb, drain hose, LED controller, control module and upper cover. Time is after bumper cover is removed.”In the Headlamp Components LED subgroup• The estimated work time of 0.7 hours applied to Headlamp Assembly is appropriate, however the footnote has been updated to state, “LABOR: Time includes R&I/R&R fan, drain hose, LED controller, control module and upper cover. Time is after bumper cover is removed.”Please note: • The part number from your concern, AU4M0941773E in not a valid OEM Part Number.• The correct OEM Part Number for the LED Headlamp Fan, located in the Headlamp Components LED subgroup, is 8U0907463A.

title: 2013 Ford Explorer — Non-Welded Panel Operations: Frame
issueSummary: CCC1 has .7 hrs. to remove and replace. These plates are welded on to the frame rails it self. These are not bolt on items. Additional time needed to weld ends on
suggestedAction: To increase labor time to pay tech fair.
resolution: Estimated Release Date: Closed Proposed Resolution: MOTOR stated: After review of MOTOR data, it has been determined that the estimated work time of 0.7 hours applied to the Frame End Plate is appropriate.Please note: Estimated Work Times published by MOTOR are compiled from several independent sources and internal processes. Those elements are then combined by the MOTOR Database Development staff and extrapolated to produce MOTOR Estimated Work Times. Each MOTOR Estimated Work Time is the product of numerous sources. Source elements relied upon are: OEM Service Manual procedures and processes, Operation Observance, OEM Warranty Times, MOTOR Historical Database Analysis, User Input, Technician Operation Familiarity Analysis, Operation Procedure Step Analysis, and Vehicle Component Configuration Analysis.Development of MOTOR Estimated Work Times requires judgments to be made by a MOTOR Database Development Staff Member while evaluating each information source and internal process. The degree to which each source element impacts the ultimate crash data varies from operation to operation as the data is normalized across the universe of the MOTOR Database. Operation times published by MOTOR are based on new undamaged parts installed on new undamaged vehicles only. Time has not been considered for rust, corrosion, collision- related damage or repairs, aftermarket parts, etc.MOTOR’s labor time development techniques and breakdowns are considered proprietary information and are not distributed externally.

title: 2017 BMW 540i — Missing Information: Electrical
issueSummary: I would like to know what time is allowed for R/I of the fuse box. Also does the R/I of the fuse box also include Fuse box 2 and 3

resolution: Per internal Audatex parts and labor procedures, Fuse Boxes, Relay Boxes and/or Junction Boxes are not supported in the database. No change warranted at this time.

title: 2017 Ford Escape SE — Procedure Page Issue: Quarter Panel

Removal of a component from a donor recycled assembly would require an on the spot evaluation.Please reference OEM1STOP for OEM repair information and position staements directly from the vehicle manufactureor Contact I-car RTS Team for any literature regarding using recycled assemblies if available.

title: 2018 GMC Sierra 1500 Denali — Parts: Front Bumper

suggestedAction: Update database to reflect part number for retainer and remove footnote that it is serviced with park sensor. Both sensor and retainer will need refinish time.
resolution: Estimated UM Release Date: 06/10/2019Proposed Resolution: MOTOR stated: After review, in the Front Bumper GMC 2016-19 group, Bumper & Components, Bumper & Hardware subgroup, the following updates have been made:• An estimated refinish time of 0.2 hours was applied to the Park Sensor.• The footnote applied to the Park Sensor has been updated to state, “LABOR: Time is after front bumper or bumper assembly is removed. Time not included with overhaul. Refinish time is for both park sensor and retainer.”Per latest provided OEM information, the Park Sensor Retainer is serviced with Park Sensor PN 84153946.

title: 2017 Volvo XC90 — Welded Panel Operations: Rear Body And Floor
issueSummary: What is included per Mitchell for welder setup and performing destructive test as outlined in the OEM manual for this vehicle? Is time to pull the welder out of the storage area, Plug in all required cables and air lines and assemble the proper arms with new welding caps included in the mitchell replacement time for the rear body panel? Does it include time for cutting test panels from the removed panel and performing destructive welds and required inspections of the test welds as outlined by the manual? Is packing the welder back up, cleaning it, moving it back into the storage area and cleaning the area it was used in for welding debris included in the panel replacement time?
suggestedAction: Clarification from mitchell and a possible addition to their database
resolution: Thank you for your inquiry. Most recent inquiry 14129 regarding this topics states Proposed Response Text:Thank you for your inquiry, please reference the following from the labor general information section of the procedure explanations:Shop Material The labor times shown in the Guide do not take into account the cost of any materials, or the cost of hazardous materials recycling or disposal.We do not provide information regarding the performance of destructive weld testing. This will be reviewed for consideration in future Procedure Page updates.Inquiry 11835 states: Thank you for your inquiry. Welding equipment set up is included in welded panel replacement. Destructive test welding is NOT INCLUDED.

title: 2019 Ford F-350 — All Other
issueSummary: There is not a labor time to replace the cab assembly
suggestedAction: Just looking for a labor time to replace the cab
resolution: Estimated Release Date: Closed Proposed Resolution: MOTOR stated: MOTOR does not develop estimated work/refinish times for the complete Cab Assembly as the Cab Assemblies make/model have too many differentiating variables and the included components cannot be determined until after an on-the-spot evaluation of the replacement Cab Assembly. MOTOR suggests using the Cab Assembly as Received, and then apply the necessary R&I, R&R and Refinish times listed in the “Crash Estimating Data” based on an on-the-spot evaluation of the replacement Body Assembly included components.

title: 2021 Tesla Model Y — Non-Welded Panel Operations: Steering Gear And Linkage
issueSummary: Insufficient labor time to replace steering gear per OEM procedure. Database labor note states: "Time is after suspension crossmember is removed". Removal of the crossmember is not required, however there are many other additional operations required including R&I of wheels, fender liners, cargo unit, cooling module assy, D&R 12v battery, disable HV, ... ect
suggestedAction: Remove incorrect labor note and adjust labor time accordingly.
resolution: Estimated UM Release Date: 10/01/2021Proposed Resolution: MOTOR stated: After Review in the Steering Gear & Linkage group, Steering Gear & Linkage subgroup, the following updates have been made:. The estimated work time applied to the Gear Assembly has been updated to 1.8 hours from 0.8 hours.. The footnote applied to the Gear Assembly has been updated to state, “LABOR: Time is after necessary bolted on parts are removed.”

title: 2018 Toyota Avalon — Parts: Grille
issueSummary: Database states "Part is no longer serviced as an assembly, please refer to listed individual components." When selecting the individual parts, message pops up that states "assembly already selected"
suggestedAction: allow users to select the individual parts without having to hand type them
resolution: Estimated UM Release Date: 01/03/2022Proposed Resolution: MOTOR stated: After review, in the Front Bumper & Grille 2016-2018 group, Grille & Components subgroup, the following updates have been made:• Updated the Grille Assembly to (5310107901).• Removed footnote from Grille Assembly that states: “PARTS: Part is no longer serviced as an assembly, please refer to listed individual components.”• Updated Upper Grille to (SEE FOOTNOTE).• Added a footnote to the Upper Grille that states: “PARTS: Part serviced with grille assembly.”

title: 2015 Chevrolet TRAX — All Other
issueSummary: Does the term Caulk/ Seam sealer in the Motor Guide to Estimating in CCC1 for rear body panel replacement include cost of seam sealer or not. This is not specific to rear body panels. Could we get clarification as it is getting read as cost of sealer included as well as labor.
suggestedAction: Note labor only
resolution: Thank you for your inquiry. While some operations may include the application of seam sealer, ANY / ALL material cost associated with repairs are NOT INCLUDED. “Labor Time Premise”/ “Estimated Work Time premise” has this listed under the NOT INCLUDED List.

title: 2020 Toyota Corolla — Non-Welded Panel Operations
issueSummary: Headliner R/I labor only allows 4.3 hours. This is not enough labor to remove as outlined in the OEM Service procedure which also requires the dash/ instrument panel to be removed. There is NO footnote in the part selection of NOT INCLUDED operation or the time is not sufficent to remove the headliner based on OE service information to remove if being reflected with removing the dash.
suggestedAction: Increase labor time to reflect the OEM service procedures or add footnote of NOT INCLUDED operations.
resolution: Proposed Response Text: Thank you for your inquiry, Labor time does not include R&I/R&R of instrument Panel. Should the user need to remove Instrument Panel, user will need to manually add to estimate. Refer to P-page #17 Included Operations: “Remove and install or replace: Rear seat, Sun visors, dome lamp, grip handles and coat hooks, Inside mouldings that interfere”. No changes at this time.

title: 2021 Ram 1500 — All Other
issueSummary: is the removal of the door seal, the clear circle tape that protects the hole to the handle bolts , disconnecting the electronics and the door weather strip included in the 03 per door handle replacement time.?
suggestedAction: Note advising what is included
resolution: Estimated Release Date: Closed Proposed Resolution: MOTOR stated: After review, the following has been determined:• Removing and installing the clear protector is Included in the estimated work time applied to the Door Handle.• Unclipping and clipping the wiring harness pig tail is Included in the estimated work time applied to the Door Handle.• R&I of the Surround Weatherstrip would be considered Not Included in the estimated work time applied to the Door Handle.

title: 2016 Ford Transit — Procedure Page Issue: Rear Body And Floor
issueSummary: On this model, the footnote for the Rear Body Panel states: "LABOR: Time is after rear bumper is removed. Time is after all necessary bolted-on parts and interior trim are removed." When the Bumper Cover is selected to Replace in conjunction with the Rear Body Panel, a "Deduct for Rear Bumper R&I" comes up with a .5 deduction.
suggestedAction: Correct the database by removing this deduction of .5 when the Bumper Cover is Replaced in conjunction with the Rear Body Panel
resolution: Estimated UM Release Date: 10/10/2022Proposed Resolution: Updated the database to show Deduct for Rear Bumper R&I not included when selecting with Rear Body Panel in the estimate, per the Labor footnote.

title: 2020 Honda Odyssey — Procedure Page Issue: Back Glass
issueSummary: Does the time to R&I urethane-bonded fixed glasses included the time to remove the existing rubber moldings/seals/spacers, clean the adhesive residue, install new moldings/seals/spacers, and fabricate a depth/recess template if needed. Some urethane-bonded fixed glasses have as many as 6 moldings and 10 spacers.
suggestedAction: Provide clarification on how many moldings/seals/spacers the time to R&I various urethane-bonded fixed glasses includes.
resolution: Web Tracking Number: 21345 Vehicle: ARG4433 2018-2023 HOND ODYSSEY Inquiry: Area of Vehicle: Back GlassPage #: Issue Summary: Does the time to R&I urethane-bonded fixed glasses included the time to remove the existing rubber moldings/seals/spacers, clean the adhesive residue, install new moldings/seals/spacers, and fabricate a depth/recess template if needed. Some urethane-bonded fixed glasses have as many as 6 moldings and 10 spacers.Suggested Action: Provide clarification on how many moldings/seals/spacers the time to R&I various urethane-bonded fixed glasses includes.Content Verification:Estimated Release Date: Closed Proposed Resolution: MOTOR stated: After review of your concern, we have determined the following:WINDSHIELD GLASS SPECIAL NOTATION: Glass, moldings, mounting studs or mounting flange may be damaged or broken during normal service procedures on some applications. MOTOR advises that these factors be considered before performing glass operations in the event unavoidable damage occurs. Time for the removal of old urethane, clean and preparation of sealing surfaces (vehicle and/or glass) should be estimated after an on-the-spot evaluation.INCLUDED: • Cowl vent panel/screen (if overlapping glass). • Rear View Mirror/Support (attached to glass). • Reveal & interior moldings/trim. • Rubber channel R&I (if installed). • Test for leaks. • Urethane adhesive application • Wiper arms.DOES NOT INCLUDE: • Cleanup of broken glass. • Cost of installation material or kit. • Refinish glass pinch weld (epoxy primer)BACK GLASS SPECIAL NOTATION: Glass, moldings, mounting studs or mounting flange may be damaged or broken during normal service procedures on some applications. MOTOR advises that these factors be considered before performing glass operations in the event unavoidable damage occurs. Time for the removal of old urethane, clean and preparation of sealing surfaces (vehicle and/or glass) should be estimated after an on-the-spot evaluation.INCLUDED: • High mounted stop lamp (if necessary). • Reveal & interior moldings / trim. • Rubber channel (if installed). • Test for leaks. • Urethane adhesive application. • Wiper arm.DOES NOT INCLUDE: • Cleanup of broken glass. • Cost of installation material or kit.Please Note: When there are more moldings than what included operations lists, an on-the-spot evaluation is recommended.

title: 2014 Tesla S — Missing Information: Electrical
issueSummary: Looking for clairification on what is included in this proceedure. Specifically if High Voltage battery Isolation (verifing that the battery pack has been isolated from the vehicle to allow work to be preformed on the High voltage components). Motor Database has no notes as to what is, and or, is not included in this operation. My understanding as that the proceedures very so much from vehicle manufacture, that this procedure only cover disconnect and reconnect and all other operation are to be evaluated on the spot?
suggestedAction: Additional notes or p-page to list included, not included operations
resolution: Estimated Release Date: Closed Proposed Resolution: MOTOR stated: After review in the Electrical group, the footnote applied to the Add If Required operation states “LABOR: Time is after battery, twelve volt is disconnected. Time is for deactivating and activating the high voltage power system as outlined in the OEM service manual.”

title: 2014 Toyota Corolla — Welded Panel Operations: Radiator Support
issueSummary: Upper tie bar R&I included in R&R Radiator. When upper tie bar is replaced the RT and LT Hood bumper brackets are included. They are welded on brackets and should not be included. Also the upper tie bar is included in R&R radiator which should not be included with weld on brackets. Hood bumper brackets require headlamps and parts to be fit to get exact fitment location before welding
suggestedAction: Remove included on Hood Bumper Brackets as needing to be welded on
resolution: Estimated UM Release Date: 10/10/2023Proposed Resolution: MOTOR stated: After review of your concern, we have determined the following:In the Radiator Support group, Radiator Support subgroup, the estimated work time applied to the Bracket has been updated to 0.5 hours from 0.3 hours.In the Radiator Support group, Radiator Support subgroup, the footnote applied to the Upper Tie Bar has been updated and now states, “PARTS: Part included with radiator support. LABOR: Time includes R&I/R&R horn and hood latch.”

title: 2018 Subaru outback — Refinish Operations: Rear Bumper
issueSummary: Bumper cover cap-57727AL00A no paint time associated with parts. they come in raw plastic and require prep unprimed part as well
suggestedAction: Bumper cover cap-57727AL00A no paint time associated with parts. they come in raw plastic and require prep unprimed part as well. Add paint time as well as not for prep unprimed part
resolution: Estimated UM Release Date: 03/01/2024Proposed Resolution: MOTOR stated: After review, in the Rear Bumper Legacy and Outback groups, Bumper & Components subgroups, an estimated refinish time of 0.2 hours has been applied to the Cap. MOTOR provides estimated work times for the preparation of “Unprimed Bumper Covers” only. MOTOR does not provide estimated work times for the preparation other “Unprimed Parts” (Moldings, Spoilers, Mirror Cover, Handles, etc.). Determining an estimated work time for “Prep Unprimed Part” will require an “on the spot evaluation”, an agreement between estimators, based on the following formula.Please note: For those situations where the “Part” is received in an “Unprimed” state, the MOTOR Guide To Estimating includes the following formula. UNPRIMED FLEXIBLE COMPONENT PREPARATION • 25% of the component’s base refinish time.• Maximum time allocation: 1.0 hoursINCLUDED: • Removal of mold-release agents as outlined by manufacturer • Masking (if required) • Application of adhesion promoterDOES NOT INCLUDE: • Correction of pre-existent surface imperfections • Material Costs

title: 2023 Kia Soul GT — Welded Panel Operations: Quarter Panel
issueSummary: Need clarification if there are sectioning procudres for a 2023 Kia Soul
suggestedAction: Section quarter panel or replace roof to replace quarter panel, please advise
resolution: Thank you for your inquiry. DEG Does not provide OEM repair information. Reference OEM repair information available from www.oem1stop.com or using OEM Repair information from www.alldata.com

title: 2019 Dodge Renegade — Missing Information: Front
issueSummary: In process scans. Insurances are not wanting to pay. SCRS recommends it
suggestedAction: Is this needed
resolution: In process scans may be necessary depending on the repair being performed and OEM repair procedure requires. Always refer back to OEM documentation to further support any required operation.

title: 2017 Porsche Cayenne — Missing Information: Front Lamps
issueSummary: This vehicle's headlamps are absent from the database. The vehicle comes with LED Lights and the part numbers for this headlights and not identified correctly in CCC.
suggestedAction: Add information for 958.941.773.D and 958.941.774.D to the database.
resolution: Estimated UM Release Date: 03/10/2025Proposed Resolution: MOTOR stated: After review in the Front Lamps group, Headlamp Components (w/LED) subgroup, updated the Headlamp to RH PN 958941774D, LH PN 958941773D.

title: 2019 Lexus RX350 — Refinish Operations: Hood
issueSummary: Please clarify the included/not included masking in the basic color coat application The included section states that Mask / close gap between adjacent panels up to foam tape (over spray)the not included section states that Cover / mask recessed edges / jambsWhat is Motors position on the difference between the two. Also is the foam tape materials included or any of the other materilas in the included operations
suggestedAction: Clarify the included operations
resolution: Estimating Tip – CCC – Masking recessed jambs and gapsEstimating Tip – CCC – Masking recessed jambs and gapsClosing the gap between two adjacent panels so that overspray does not get on the jamb, edge, underside, or interior is included in the Basic Color Coat Application.This is specific to two or more panels in the closed position. If a simple back-tape or the use of foam between panels to stop any overspray is not sufficient and a complete masking of the edge, jamb, underside or interior must be done, then this would not be included. These would be considered recessed edges.Per the GTE, any materials / consumables is NOT INCLUDED

title: 2021 Honda CRV — Procedure Page Issue: Front Bumper
issueSummary: On this model, once the Front Bumper Cover is selected to Replace then the Absorbers &/or Reinforcement Bar are selected, the database shows them "Inc" The footnote with the Front Bumper footnote does not list the Absorbers or the Impact Bar in the included labor list. The footnote for the Absorber & Impact Bar state: "LABOR: Time is after bumper cover is removed. Time not included with overhaul."
suggestedAction: Correct the database to show the published time for working with the Absorbers & the Impact Bar when selected in conjunction with the Bumper Cover Replacement
resolution: Estimated UM Release Date: 10/01/2025Proposed Resolution: Updated the database to show Upper and Lower Absorbers NOT included with Bumper R&R and Overhaul per the Labor footnotes.Please note: The VIN provided was for a Nissan, and the vehicle details provided was for a Honda. The issue was identified and corrected for the Nissan.

title: 2019 Subaru Forester Touring — Non-Welded Panel Operations: Cooling
issueSummary: Based on DEG Inq 20745 and the applicable OEM service information, the labor time provided to R&I for the radiator is too low. Considering INQ 20745 is for a 2013 Legacy, and the bleed procedure is slightly less involved than that of the 2019 Forester, it would be expected that the applicable labor time would be equal to or greater than the 3.1 for automatic transmission. Additionally, the use of the Subaru Select Monitor diagnostic tool is required, which, following past logic, would be non-included and a separate consideration to hook up and operate the coolant purge function during the bleeding process. Consideration for any duplicate efforts would be an on-the-spot evaluation as provided in the past.
suggestedAction: Review and adjust the Radiator R&I labor time to at least 3.1 and include a note that bleeding is following the OEM procedure, and use of any diagnostic tooling would be non-included.
resolution: Estimated UM Release Date: 12/08/2025Proposed Resolution: MOTOR stated: After review, in the Cooling group, Radiator & Components subgroup, the estimated work time applied to the Radiator has been updated to 3.5 hours from 2.8 hours.Please Note: The estimated work time does not include scan tool usage.
